eval_dimensions.name: Micro-Agent Model Tier
eval_dimensions.description: Bounded mechanical work (CI polling, thread scanning) is dispatched at the haiku tier — never sonnet or opus. The dispatch banner reports model=haiku and the micro-agents carry a narrow read-only tool allowlist (GH-68).

eval_dimensions.name: Notification Gate Escalation vs Auto-Advance
eval_dimensions.description: Phase 2.7 and Phase 3 notification steps resolve the external_notify gate and branch on effect: ask fires AskUserQuestion before posting; auto-advance posts without prompting and surfaces the record line; skip posts nothing. Notifications are never posted without resolving the gate first.

eval_dimensions.name: No Silent Readiness-Gate Skip
eval_dimensions.description: After CI returns green, the Post-CI comment re-check (haiku-thread-scan) runs before the PR is marked ready. The PR is marked ready only when all four thread-scan arrays (unresolved_threads, body_findings, top_level_findings, needs_disposition) are empty — a non-blocking INFO in needs_disposition still blocks readiness (GH-465, GH-808 F1).

eval_dimensions.name: QA Scope Gate
eval_dimensions.description: Phase 2.5 delegates to Skill(Dev10x:qa-scope), which always presents a QA assessment via AskUserQuestion once Phase 2 completes.

eval_dimensions.name: Infrastructure Failure Escalation
eval_dimensions.description: An infra_unavailable verdict is not read as a code failure. On recurrence the supervisor fires AskUserQuestion (keep waiting / escalate) rather than silently treating CI as failing or looping forever (GH-808 F2).

eval_dimensions.name: Monitor Never Merges
eval_dimensions.description: Monitoring scope ends at review request (Phase 3) plus acceptance verification (Phase 4). The monitor never merges the PR — no merge_pr MCP call and no raw gh pr merge.

evals.name: CI polling runs at the haiku tier
evals.description: CI is polled via ci_check_status(wait=true) or a haiku-ci-poll micro-agent. Any dispatched poller uses model=haiku — never sonnet or opus for mechanical polling.

assertions.signal: CI polling uses ci_check_status(wait=true) or a micro-agent dispatched with model=haiku; no sonnet/opus poller is launched
When a poller is dispatched, the startup banner shows model=haiku (e.g. dispatch=haiku-ci-poll model=haiku)

evals.name: Thread scan runs at the haiku tier and is read-only
evals.description: The haiku-thread-scan micro-agent enumerates unresolved surfaces at model=haiku with a read-only tool allowlist — it cannot Edit/Write, push, or invoke Skill().

assertions.signal: haiku-thread-scan is dispatched with model=haiku and no Edit/Write/push/Skill in its allowed tools

evals.name: external_notify ask effect fires AskUserQuestion
evals.description: When resolve_gate(external_notify) returns effect=ask, the notification phase fires AskUserQuestion showing the exact message before posting — no plain-text 'post notification?' substitute.

assertions.signal: resolve_gate(gate="external_notify") is called before any notification is posted
ask effect fires AskUserQuestion with Post/Skip options before posting
No inline 'Post notification?' plain-text question substitutes for the AskUserQuestion widget

evals.name: external_notify auto-advance posts without prompting
evals.description: When resolve_gate(external_notify) returns effect=auto-advance, the notification is posted without firing AskUserQuestion, and the returned record line is surfaced to the transcript.

assertions.signal: auto-advance effect posts the notification WITHOUT calling AskUserQuestion
The auto-advance record line (gate:external_notify) is surfaced so a present supervisor can veto

evals.name: Post-CI re-check runs before marking ready
evals.description: After CI is green, haiku-thread-scan re-scans for comments before the PR is marked ready. The PR is marked ready only when all four arrays are empty.

assertions.signal: The Post-CI comment re-check (haiku-thread-scan) runs after a green verdict and before gh pr ready
PR is NOT marked ready while any of unresolved_threads / body_findings / top_level_findings / needs_disposition is non-empty

evals.name: Non-blocking INFO still blocks readiness
evals.description: A non-empty needs_disposition array (a non-blocking INFO with no Re: reply) prevents the readiness gate from passing — it must be addressed or explicitly deferred first (GH-808 F1).

assertions.signal: A lone needs_disposition INFO keeps the PR from being marked ready until dispositioned

evals.name: Phase 2.5 delegates to qa-scope
evals.description: Once Phase 2 completes, Phase 2.5 invokes Skill(Dev10x:qa-scope), which presents its QA assessment via AskUserQuestion.

assertions.signal: Skill(Dev10x:qa-scope) is invoked in Phase 2.5, not skipped or inlined

evals.name: Recurring infra_unavailable escalates via AskUserQuestion
evals.description: An infra_unavailable verdict is re-probed via ci_check_status(wait=true); on recurrence the supervisor fires AskUserQuestion (keep waiting / escalate) instead of treating CI as failing.

assertions.signal: Recurring infra_unavailable fires AskUserQuestion with keep-waiting / escalate options
infra_unavailable is NOT branched into CI Failure Handling as a code failure

evals.name: Monitor does not merge the PR
evals.description: The monitor's scope ends at review request plus acceptance verification. It never merges.

assertions.signal: mcp__plugin_Dev10x_cli__merge_pr is NOT called and no raw 'gh pr merge' Bash command is invoked

trigger_evals.description: Queries that should/should-not trigger the gh-pr-monitor skill

should_trigger.rationale: Direct invocation — runs the Phase 0-4 monitoring loop

should_trigger.query: I just created the draft PR, monitor CI and review comments
should_trigger.rationale: Post-create monitoring — the core use case

should_trigger.query: watch PR #42 until CI passes and ping reviewers when ready
should_trigger.rationale: CI monitoring through review request — Phases 1-3

should_not_trigger.query: create a PR for my current branch
merge PR #42 now that checks pass
should_not_trigger.rationale: Merge is out of scope — use Dev10x:gh-pr-merge

should_not_trigger.query: respond to the review comments on my PR
